text.title: Cross-Channel Budget Allocation
text.summary: Comparing efficiency across Meta, Google, and LinkedIn; incrementality consideration; reallocation examples.

rule.statement: Compare channels by efficiency ratio, not absolute CPA — a $100 LinkedIn CPA may outperform a $50 Meta CPA if LinkedIn leads close at 5x the rate.
rule.rationale: Different channels reach different funnel stages and audience quality levels. Raw CPA ignores downstream conversion quality and LTV differences.

example.code: Channel Spend Conv CPA ROAS Efficiency (target $60) Meta $5K 100 $50 4:1 1.20 → Scale Google $3K 50 $60 3:1 1.00 → Hold LinkedIn $2K 20 $100 1.5:1 0.60 → Reduce or optimize

rule.statement: Before cutting a channel, test incrementality — pause it for 2 weeks and measure whether total conversions decline, not just that channel's reported conversions.
rule.rationale: Platform attribution overlaps; cutting a channel with reported poor CPA may remove touches that were assisting conversions attributed to other channels.

list.item: High incrementality: conversions drop when channel is paused → keep and optimize
Low incrementality: conversions hold when channel is paused → safe to reallocate

example.code: Current: Meta $5K, Google $3K, LinkedIn $2K — total $10K, target CPA $60 Meta: $50 CPA, 20% under target → increase to $6K (+$1K) Google: $75 CPA, 25% over target → decrease to $2.5K (-$500) LinkedIn: $133 CPA, 122% over target → decrease to $1K (-$1K) Testing reserve: +$500 for new channel test New total: $10K maintained
